folders.name: Introductory
folders.description: Drills for the table tennis player just starting out

drills.name: Forehand Warmup
drills.description: Warmup drill with topspin serve to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Warmup
drills.description: Warmup drill with topspin serve to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand 2-Step Footwork
drills.description: Footwork drill with two serves to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand 2-Step Footwork
drills.description: Footwork drill with two serves to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand Push
drills.description: Warmup drill with backspin serve to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Push
drills.description: Warmup drill with backspin serve to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand Push With Footwork
drills.description: Footwork drill with backspin serve to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Push With Footwork
drills.description: Footword drill with backspin serve to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand Topspin
drills.description: Simple topspin ball to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Topspin
drills.description: Simple topspin ball to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand Topspin With Footwork
drills.description: Footwork with topspin balls to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Topspin With Footwork
drills.description: Footwork with topspin balls to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand Backspin
drills.description: Simple backspin ball to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Backspin
drills.description: Simple backspin ball to the backhand

drills.name: Forehand Backspin With Footwork
drills.description: Footwork with backspin balls to the forehand

drills.name: Backhand Backspin With Footwork
drills.description: Footwork with backspin balls to the backhand

drills.name: Three Serve Balls
drills.description: Three serves - topspin, no spin, and backspin

drills.name: Three Balls
drills.description: Three balls - topspin, no spin, and backspin

drills.name: No Spin Forehand
drills.description: No spin to the forehand

drills.name: No Spin Backhand
drills.description: No spin to the backhand

folders.name: Beginner
folders.description: Drills for the table tennis player just starting out

drills.name: Beginner Forehand Warmup
drills.description: Warm-up your basic forehand stroke by keeping your angle set at 90 degrees and stroking forward on the ball.

drills.name: Beginner Backhand Warmup
drills.description: Warm-up your backhand stroke by controlling the ball to different locations   sometimes hitting to the forehand, sometimes middle, and sometimes backhand.

drills.name: Forehand Backhand Footwork
drills.description: Link the strokes together by hitting one forehand and one backhand! Use a mini-step to move from one ball to the next.

drills.name: Beginner Forehand Random
drills.description: This drill is a bit more challenging. The robot will move the ball around in the forehand zone; watch the robot s head and look for the subtle variations!

drills.name: Beginner Backhand Random
drills.description: This drill will give you balls in the backhand zone. Keep your racket in front, move into position, and control the ball sometimes hitting a bit harder, sometimes giving a slow variation!

drills.name: Beginner Full Table Random
drills.description: Now this feels more like a game! Just like playing a real opponent, the balls will go anywhere on the table. Work on adjusting to the ball, while still maintaining good ball placement on all of your shots.

drills.name: Serve Then Two Random
drills.description: This drill gives you a serve then two balls anywhere, then a break, just like a game.

drills.name: In-And-Out Footwork
drills.description: Move in for the short forehand, then move back for the deep forehand. Next, move in for the short backhand, then move back a bit for the deep backhand. Stay on your toes, leaning forward, and contact the ball in-front of your body.

drills.name: Three Middle, One to Either Corner
drills.description: Pay Attention! The first three balls go to your middle, then suddenly the fourth balls goes surprisingly to either corner. Watch the robot s head and be ready to make the adjustment!

drills.name: Forehand Random
drills.description: Within the forehand side of the table, you will get medium speed balls. Move quickly, get in position, and turn your shoulders as you make contact with the ball!

drills.name: Backhand Random
drills.description: Within the backhand side of the table, you will get medium speed balls. Lean forward, creating space for your backspin, and brush the ball forward using your wrist and forearm to generate spin on your backhand stroke.

drills.name: Fast Random
drills.description: Be ready to quickly adjust, these balls will be coming anywhere on the table. Keep your racket in front and be ready to make fast transitions from middle to forehand to backhand!

drills.name: Short Forehand, Deep Backhand
drills.description: The first ball will be short to your forehand, then the follow-up ball will be deep to your backhand. If you are right handed, step forward with your right foot to get you closer to the ball.

drills.name: Short Backhand, Deep Forehand
drills.description: Attack the first ball with your backhand, then quickly get back for the deep forehand.

drills.name: Backhand Rally, Surprise Forehand
drills.description: This is just like a game. It starts with a serve, then gives a backhand rally of one to five balls, then suddenly it surprises you to the forehand. After the sequence, it gives a short break for you to physically and mentally recover for the next ball.

drills.name: No-Spin Serves
drills.description: This drill gives you no-spin serves. You can choose whether to chop or attack each of these balls. Whichever you choose, make sure that you keep a relaxed grip and brush the ball to impart maximum spin.

drills.name: Serve, Two Speed Balls
drills.description: After returning the serve, get ready for two fast balls. Just meet the ball and focus on keeping it low!

drills.name: Serve, High Forehand, High Backhand
drills.description: After returning the serve, you will get a high ball to your forehand then a high ball to the backhand. Get in position and smash both high balls with your powerful forehand!

drills.name: Serve, Then Speed
drills.description: After returning the serve, be ready for three fast balls   one to the forehand, one to the middle, then one to the backhand.

drills.name: Forehand Game Situation
drills.description: First, you will get a backspin serve to the forehand, push it. Next you will get a deep backspin ball to the forehand, get down low and loop it up and over the net. Finally, you will get a deep topspin ball to the forehand, start your hand high and drive through the ball forward.

drills.name: Backhand Game Situation
drills.description: First, you will get a backspin serve to the backhand, push it. Next you will get a deep backspin ball to the backhand, get down low and loop it up and over the net. Finally, you will get a deep topspin ball to the backhand, start your hand high and drive through the ball forward.

drills.name: Short Serve Then 10 Balls
drills.description: You will get a short serve, step forward and push or flip this ball. Next, you will have a marathon rally of 10 topspin balls anywhere on the table. You will then have a short rest before the next rally.

drills.name: No Spin, Topspin
drills.description: Watch out for the tricky spin variations. The robot will alternate between no-spin serves and topspin serves! Pay attention and adjust your swing slightly for each ball!

drills.name: Left Sidespin Serve Return
drills.description: Regardless if you choose to hit with your forehand or backhand, touch the right side of the ball to control it onto the table.

drills.name: Right Sidespin Serve Return
drills.description: If you contact the back of the ball, it will fly off your racket far to the left. In order to counter the spin, touch the left side of the ball.

drills.name: No-Spin Serve Return
drills.description: Against these serves, float the ball controlled over the net with a steady chop.

drills.name: Topspin Serve Return
drills.description: Using a controlled stroke, contact the ball with a relaxed grip and a 60 degree racket angle.

drills.name: Left Sidespin Serve Anywhere
drills.description: Be ready to adjust in-and-out as well as side-to-side for these very spinny left sidespin serves.

drills.name: Right Sidespin Serve Anywhere
drills.description: Prepare for the long sidespin serve, but always be ready to move in slightly with the right foot for the surprise short one! To control the spin, touch the left side of the ball.

drills.name: Left Sidespin Topspin Serve
drills.description: To return this combo serve, contact the top right of the ball. If you imagine that the ball is a globe with the United States facing you, you will touch the state of Maine.

drills.name: Right Sidespin Topspin Serve
drills.description: To return this combo serve, contact the top left of the ball. This serve is a simulation of a right handed player doing a backhand serve.

drills.name: Backspin, Topspin
drills.description: After getting a backspin serve, you will then get five topspin balls anywhere. Try to keep score against the robot. Every time you get all 6 balls back, it is 1 point for you. Every time you miss during the six ball sequence, it is a point for Robo-Pong. Games to 11. Loser buys lunch.

folders.name: Intermediate
folders.description: Drills for the intermediate table tennis player

drills.name: Intermediate Forehand Warmup
drills.description: Contact the top of the ball and swing forward toward the top of the net.

drills.name: Intermediate Backhand Warmup
drills.description: Use a quick, short stroke and get ready for a series of quick balls to your backhand.

drills.name: Two Backhands, Two Forehands
drills.description: Practice varying your spin a bit. Hit the first ball at medium speed, then the next a bit faster. Alternate speeds between each hit   medium backhand, hard backhand, medium forehand, hard forehand.

drills.name: Forehand, Middle
drills.description: What a workout! Continuously move for every ball, playing one forehand from the forehand side then one forehand from the middle!

drills.name: Backhand, Middle
drills.description: Backhand footwork is one of the most neglected aspects of the game! Use your backhand from the backhand side then quickly move and use your backhand from the middle of the table.

drills.name: Backhand, Middle, Backhand, Forehand
drills.description: This systematic drill alternates between backhand and forehand with a unique pattern   backhand, middle, backhand, forehand!

drills.name: One or Two Backhands, One or Two Forehands
drills.description: I love this semi-systematic drill. Watch the robot s head to see where the ball is going, then focus on the ball as it approaches.

drills.name: Middle, Either Corner
drills.description: This is one of the world s most popular drills! Play a forehand from the middle, then be ready to adjust for the surprise to either corner. The pattern continues, one middle then one to either corner. See if you can last for three full minutes!

drills.name: Wide Faulkenberg
drills.description: This drill gives you two balls to the backhand then one ball to the wide forehand. Play the first ball with your backhand, play the second ball with your forehand from the backhand side, play the final ball with your forehand from the forehand side.

drills.name: Forehand Random
drills.description: You will be getting speed balls anywhere in the forehand side of the table. Take mini-steps and continue to move for every ball!

drills.name: Backhand Random
drills.description: Your choice, you can use all backhand or you can choose to use both backhand and forehand for this drill. As you improve, you can also vary the speed, spin, and placement of your shots!

drills.name: Random
drills.description: The fast topspin balls will now go anywhere on the table. Be ready to move, especially for the tricky middle ball. Keep your feet active, stay on your toes, and be ready to adjust!

drills.name: Short Forehand, Deep Backhand
drills.description: After getting a short backspin ball to the forehand, you will then get two quick topspin balls to the backhand. Push or flip the short one, then loop the two deep ones.

drills.name: Serve Then Footwork
drills.description: After getting a short backspin ball to the backhand, you will then get three deep topspin balls   one to the forehand, one to the middle, and one to the backhand. After these four balls, you will get a short rest before the next sequence.

drills.name: Three Ball Drill
drills.description: You will get a short backspin serve to the middle, followed by a heavy backspin ball to either corner, followed by a spinny topspin ball back to the middle!

drills.name: Three Ball Random
drills.description: This is the same as the previous drill, but now all three balls are random! Short backspin then long backspin then long topspin!

drills.name: Twelve Ball Rally
drills.description: It all starts with a short backspin serve followed by a long backspin push, then the marathon begins   ten fast topspins anywhere. It s like playing a blocker who never misses!

drills.name: No Spin, Topspin, High Ball
drills.description: This is what the 3050 XL is all about   Game-like variations! You will get a sequence of a no-spin balls, then a topspin ball, then a high ball.

drills.name: Left Sidespin Then Rally
drills.description: After returning the left sidespin serve, be prepared for three or four or five fast topspin balls. Keep track of the score, every time you have a perfect rally, you get one point. Every time you miss a ball during the rally, the robot gets one point! Games to 11! I never said he was an easy opponent!

drills.name: Serve Then Backhand Random
drills.description: After returning the right sidespin serve, you will then get a tough rally of ten balls randomly to your backhand!

drills.name: Serve Then Speed
drills.description: Attack the topspin serve then block the next two speed balls. To control your block, relax your grip and keep your hand in-front of your body and cushion the ball with a pillow grip.

drills.name: High Spins
drills.description: This difficult five ball drill gives you a serve, then a high backspin, high topspin, high backspin, high topspin. Smash the backspin by going up and forward. Smash the topspin by going down and forward.

drills.name: High Backhand, High Forehand
drills.description: Use your forehand to smash both balls   one high ball from the backhand side and one high ball from the forehand side.

drills.name: High Spins Smashing
drills.description: Smash the high topspin ball by hitting slightly down and forward. Smash the high backspin ball by hitting slightly up and forward. This drill has a lot more spin than you might guess   it is like playing a high level chopper!

drills.name: Forehand Speed
drills.description: The lightening speed balls to the forehand come fast and with a bit of topspin. Get close to the table and contact the ball early, on the rise.

drills.name: Backhand Speed
drills.description: To block these speed balls, crouch low in your ready position, keep your racket in front of your body, and block with control keeping the ball low.

drills.name: Left Sidespin Topspin Serve
drills.description: The left sidespin topspin serve is like a right handed player serving a forehand pendulum serve. To return this difficult spin, touch the top right side of the ball.

drills.name: Right Sidespin Topspin Serve
drills.description: The right sidespin topspin serve is like a left handed player serving a forehand pendulum serve. To return this difficult spin, touch the top left side of the ball.

drills.name: Left Sidespin Backspin Serve
drills.description: To return this serve, you can either push it with control or loop it with spin. Touch the bottom right side of the ball.

drills.name: Right Sidespin Backspin Serve
drills.description: rTo return this serve, push or loop with spin. It is vitally important to impart your own spin so that your opponent s spin will have less effect on your rubber. Work on stroking through the ball slightly.

drills.name: Smash, Topspin, Lob
drills.description: You will first get a speed ball, block it with control. Next, you will get a normal topspin ball, loop it with good topspin. Finally, you will get a high ball, smash it with every ounce of energy you have left!

drills.name: Spinny Serves
drills.description: This drill will give you very spinny serves anywhere long! Look at the lights to tell the direction and amount of spin. The more lights that are on, the stronger the spin.

folders.name: Advanced
folders.description: Drills for the advanced table tennis player

drills.name: Advanced Forehand Warmup
drills.description: These balls to the forehand are not only fast, but also have placement, speed, spin, height, and depth variations. Be ready to make quick adjustments!

drills.name: Advanced Backhand Warmup
drills.description: With fast and slightly random balls to the backhand, the advanced backhand warmup is both a fun and challenging beginning to any practice session. Warmup for one minute, take a break, recover, then warmup for another minute.

drills.name: Forehand, Middle, Backhand
drills.description: Within the forehand, middle, and backhand zones, the robot will add subtle variations. Sometimes challenge yourself to see if you can play all forehands the entire time!

drills.name: One or Two Forehands, One or Two Backhands
drills.description: Using all forehand, make quick adjustments to hit forehand from the wide angle and forehands from the center of the table.

drills.name: One or Two Backhands, One or Two Middle
drills.description: You can use backhand or forehand for this drill, just make sure that you are moving for every ball. Also, watch the robot s head to see where the next ball with be thrown.

drills.name: One Middle, One Random
drills.description: First you will get a speed ball to the middle, next you will get a speed ball anywhere, the pattern continues, one middle, one random.

drills.name: Forehand, Backhand, Random
drills.description: This is called a semi-systematic drill. First a simple forehand then backhand followed by a surprise anywhere!

drills.name: Six Balls
drills.description: It starts with a backhand, middle, forehand, then goes random for three balls anywhere. The pattern continues without a break. Try to survive for two full minutes before taking a needed water break!

drills.name: Backhand, Random
drills.description: This drill alternates between a backhand then a random ball anywhere. Remember, the random ball might just be to the backhand; in that case, you would get three backhands in a row.

drills.name: Nine Balls
drills.description: Three backhands, three forehands, then three random. Try your best to change locations when hitting, sometimes hit to the wide forehand, sometimes wide backhand, and sometimes try to jam your opponent in the middle.

drills.name: Medium Random
drills.description: This drill always keeps you guessing, it gives excellent variations in placement and actually feels like you are playing a real game!

drills.name: Wild Random
drills.description: This tough drill gives you placement, speed, spin, height, and depth variations. Be alert and ready to make the adjustments. Start off with just doing twenty balls, before taking a break. As you develop the ability to adjust, then challenge yourself with longer drills.

drills.name: Serve, Backspin, Topspin
drills.description: First, the serve will come with slight backspin sidespin, next a very spinny backspin, followed by a very spinny topspin ball. Be ready to adjust to the extreme spin!

drills.name: Serve, Forehand, Backhand
drills.description: This is a great drill for defense. It gives you a backspin serve, followed by a topspin ball to your forehand then a topspin ball to your backhand.

drills.name: No-Spin Serve then Backhand, Forehand
drills.description: After pushing the no-spin serve, use a controlled stroke and block with your backhand then block with your forehand. Try to keep your blocks as low to the net as possible!

drills.name: Serve Then Middle Random
drills.description: After a heavy backspin serve, the footwork drill begins   middle then random, middle then random, middle then random. Try to play most of the middle balls with your forehand.

drills.name: Block Training
drills.description: After returning the no-spin serve, you will then get five to ten topspin balls anywhere, simply block the ball with control. See if you can last the entire drill without missing a single block!

drills.name: Mega Backspin, Ten Topspins
drills.description: After pushing the very spinny serve, endure the ten ball rally with your steady defense. Instead of reaching for the balls, move your feet into position for each shot   it is tiring, but worth the effort!

drills.name: Two Ball Speed
drills.description: This is the quickest drill on the robot. After a left sidespin serve, it will immediately give you a quick topspin ball.

drills.name: Serve Then Rally of One-Ten Balls
drills.description: After returning the left sidespin serve, be ready for a variable rally of one to ten balls. Sometimes it will just give one, sometimes six, sometimes maybe ten. Prepare for the ten every time.

drills.name: No-Spin Serve Then Speed
drills.description: This is one of the most entertaining drills! After returning the no-spin serve, the robot will pound you hard with the next shot. Just try to make contact with the ball. As you become more advanced, then you can begin taking a small swing.

drills.name: Serve Then Ten Lobs
drills.description: You will be winded, guaranteed! After returning the topspin serve, you will get ten lobs. Get in position, and smash down and forward with full power!

drills.name: Speed Forehand, Speed Backhand
drills.description: The robot will give you one speed ball randomly in the forehand zone then one speed ball randomly in the backhand zone. Move into position and block the ball with good placement.

drills.name: Random Smashing
drills.description: Speed balls will be coming anywhere on the table. Instead of merely guessing, you should keep your racket neutral in-front, move into position, and try to touch every ball. Even if you can t block it on, try to make contact.

drills.name: Random Lobbing
drills.description: The most difficult part about these lobs is the depth variation. Be ready to move out and smash or be ready to charge the table and smash. As the ball leaves the robot s head, try to judge the depth and move accordingly!

drills.name: Spin Lobs
drills.description: This drill will give you a topspin lob, backspin lob, then a no-spin lob. Instead of lobbing, it will sometimes be a bit low and short, in this case, give it a controlled stroke. If it is high, try to use your most powerful smash!

drills.name: Sidespin Lobs
drills.description: Move the furniture aside! This drill requires a bit of space! The sidespin lobs will be curving at very wide angles! Read the spin, adjust with your feet, and smash all the balls using your forehand!

drills.name: Short and Long
drills.description: The robot will give you one to four short backspin balls followed by one deep backspin ball followed by three deep topspin balls to the backhand then one deep topspin ball to the forehand.

drills.name: Short Ball Training
drills.description: After one to ten short backspin balls, the robot will surprise long with a backspin ball anywhere, followed by a long topspin ball anywhere.

drills.name: Backspin, No-Spin Serve
drills.description: This drill alternates between a short backspin serve and a short no-spin serve. Push the backspin and flip the no-spin. Also, work on placement by sometimes returning short, sometimes long, sometimes to the middle, sometimes use the angles.

drills.name: Heavy Topspins
drills.description: This drill gives you heavy topspin balls to your forehand on half of the table. Practice your blocking placement to various locations. As you perfect your control, then begin an active block or even a counterloop!

drills.name: Low Lobs
drills.description: This drill gives you lower lobs   one topspin, one backspin, then one no-spin. Move into position and smash all of them using your forehand.

drills.name: Topspin to either corner
drills.description: Topspin to either corner

drills.name: Backhand topspin rally, the surprise forehand
drills.description: Backhand topspin rally, the surprise forehand

drills.name: Topspin small movements
drills.description: Topspin small movements

drills.name: Forehand topspin moving toward backhand
drills.description: Forehand topspin moving toward backhand

drills.name: Spinny variation short serves
drills.description: Spinny variation short serves

drills.name: Very spinny backspin serve
drills.description: Very spinny backspin serve

drills.name: One Topspin, One Sidespin
drills.description: One Topspin, One Sidespin

drills.name: Random placement one topspin, one sidespin
drills.description: Random placement one topspin, one sidespin

drills.name: Weird sidespin rally
drills.description: Weird sidespin rally

drills.name: Spinny Long Serves
drills.description: This drill will give you very spinny serves to either corner! Look at the lights to tell the direction and amount of spin. The more lights that are on, the stronger the spin.

folders.name: 30-Day Program
folders.description: Drills for the 30-Day Challenge training program

drills.name: BH Block Slow
drills.description: Slow topspin to backhand for block technique work

drills.name: BH Block Medium
drills.description: Medium speed topspin to backhand for block progression

drills.name: BH Block Fast
drills.description: Fast topspin to backhand for reaction block training

drills.name: FH-BH Block
drills.description: Alternating topspin to forehand and backhand for block footwork

drills.name: Short Backspin FH Flick
drills.description: Short backspin to forehand for strawberry flick practice

drills.name: Short Backspin BH Flick
drills.description: Short backspin to backhand for banana flick practice

drills.name: Short No-Spin FH
drills.description: Short no-spin ball to forehand for flick practice

drills.name: Short No-Spin BH
drills.description: Short no-spin ball to backhand for flick practice

drills.name: Short Sidespin L BH
drills.description: Short left sidespin to backhand for banana flick with spin read

drills.name: Short Sidespin R FH
drills.description: Short right sidespin to forehand for strawberry flick with spin read

drills.name: Mixed Short Serves
drills.description: Alternating backspin and no-spin short serves for read and react

drills.name: BH Topspin Consistency
drills.description: Steady medium topspin to backhand for rally consistency

drills.name: Backspin Open Attack
drills.description: Heavy backspin to center for opening topspin attack practice

drills.name: BH Flick Then Rally
drills.description: Short backspin then topspin rally to backhand — flick into rally

drills.name: FH Flick Then Rally
drills.description: Short backspin then topspin rally to forehand — flick into rally

drills.name: Quick Reaction Random
drills.description: Fast topspin balls with random placement for reaction training

drills.name: Sidespin Serve Rally
drills.description: Sidespin serve followed by topspin rally — serve receive into attack

drills.name: Mixed Spin Serves
drills.description: Backspin, no-spin, topspin serves in rotation for spin read training

drills.name: Game Pattern Serve-Flick-Rally
drills.description: Short serve → flick → 3 topspins — full game sequence simulation

drills.name: Sidespin Mixed LR
drills.description: Alternating left and right sidespin serves for spin read practice

drills.name: FH Topspin vs No-Spin
drills.description: No-spin ball to forehand — practice opening topspin on flat ball

drills.name: BH Topspin vs No-Spin
drills.description: No-spin ball to backhand — practice opening topspin on flat ball

drills.name: FH Topspin vs Backspin
drills.description: Backspin ball to forehand — practice opening topspin on backspin

drills.name: BH Topspin vs Backspin
drills.description: Backspin ball to backhand — practice opening topspin on backspin

drills.name: FH Counter-Topspin
drills.description: Topspin ball to forehand — practice counter-topspin over the table

drills.name: BH Counter-Topspin
drills.description: Topspin ball to backhand — practice counter-topspin over the table

drills.name: FH-BH Topspin Alternating
drills.description: Alternating topspin to forehand and backhand for transition practice

drills.name: Mixed Spin FH Open
drills.description: No-spin → backspin → topspin to forehand — read spin and react

drills.name: In-Out FH Attack
drills.description: Chinese pattern: short backspin → deep topspin to FH — in-and-out footwork

drills.name: In-Out BH Attack
drills.description: Chinese pattern: short backspin → deep topspin to BH — in-and-out footwork

drills.name: 3rd Ball FH
drills.description: Chinese 3rd ball: serve → push to FH → FH topspin attack

drills.name: 3rd Ball BH
drills.description: Chinese 3rd ball: serve → push to BH → BH topspin attack

drills.name: 4th Ball Counter FH
drills.description: Chinese 4th ball: short → topspin → block FH → FH counter-topspin

drills.name: 4th Ball Counter BH
drills.description: Chinese 4th ball: short → topspin → block BH → BH counter-topspin

drills.name: FH Topspin Everywhere
drills.description: Chinese pattern: FH topspin across full table with wide random placement

drills.name: Serve-Flick-Counter
drills.description: Chinese pattern: short serve → flick → fast counter — 3 ball game sequence
